meta.title: Daily Reflection Tree
meta.description: An end-of-day structured reflection across three psychological axes: Locus of Control, Orientation, and Radius of Concern.

nodes.text:
Good evening. Before the day closes, let's sit with it for a moment.

This isn't a performance review. There's no right answer. Just honest reflection — yours, for you.
If today were a weather report, what would it be?
options: Sunny — things went well, I felt in control
Overcast — okay, but something felt heavy
Stormy — things went sideways more than once
Foggy — I'm not sure what even happened
answer=Sunny — things went well, I felt in control:A1_Q_HIGH
answer=Overcast — okay, but something felt heavy:A1_Q_MID
answer=Stormy — things went sideways more than once:A1_Q_LOW
answer=Foggy — I'm not sure what even happened:A1_Q_LOW

nodes.text: You said today felt sunny. When things went well, what do you think made the difference?
options: I prepared — I knew what I was walking into
I adapted when the plan changed
The team or circumstances lined up well
I pushed through something I would've avoided before

nodes.text: You said today felt overcast. That heaviness — where did it mostly come from?
options: Something I didn't handle as well as I could have
Waiting on someone else who didn't come through
I wasn't sure what was expected of me
A decision I made that I'm second-guessing

nodes.text: When things went sideways today, what was your first instinct?
options: Find what I could still control and focus there
Figure out who or what caused it
Push through alone without asking for help
Wait for things to settle before acting
answer=I prepared — I knew what I was walking into:A1_R_INTERNAL
answer=I adapted when the plan changed:A1_R_INTERNAL
answer=The team or circumstances lined up well:A1_R_EXTERNAL
answer=I pushed through something I would've avoided before:A1_R_INTERNAL
answer=Something I didn't handle as well as I could have:A1_R_INTERNAL
answer=Waiting on someone else who didn't come through:A1_R_EXTERNAL
answer=I wasn't sure what was expected of me:A1_R_EXTERNAL
answer=A decision I made that I'm second-guessing:A1_R_INTERNAL
answer=Find what I could still control and focus there:A1_R_INTERNAL
answer=Figure out who or what caused it:A1_R_EXTERNAL
answer=Push through alone without asking for help:A1_R_INTERNAL
answer=Wait for things to settle before acting:A1_R_EXTERNAL

nodes.text:
There's a thread running through your day — you kept orienting back to yourself. Not selfishly, but as someone who believes their actions matter.

That's agency. It doesn't mean everything went your way. It means you stayed in the driver's seat, even when the road was rough.

Today, your attention drifted outward — to what others did or didn't do, to what circumstances allowed or blocked.

That's human. But before we move on: somewhere in today, you made a call. Even a small one. What was it?
Let's shift gears. We've looked at how you handled today. Now let's look at what you gave.
Think about your energy today — where did most of it flow?
options: Into my own work and goals
Into helping or unblocking someone else
Into defending my work or getting recognition
Into something the team needed, not just me
answer=Into my own work and goals:A2_Q_SELF
answer=Into helping or unblocking someone else:A2_Q_CONTRIB
answer=Into defending my work or getting recognition:A2_Q_ENTITLE
answer=Into something the team needed, not just me:A2_Q_CONTRIB

nodes.text: You gave energy to others today. Was that something you chose — or something that was expected of you?
options: I chose it — no one asked me to
Someone needed help and I stepped up without thinking
It was expected, but I gave more than required
Honestly, I'm not sure — it just happened

nodes.text: Your energy went into your own work today. At the end of it, how did you feel about what you received in return?
options: Fair — I put in effort and it showed
Underappreciated — I expected more acknowledgment
Neutral — I wasn't tracking it
Like I could have done more for others too

nodes.text: You were focused on recognition today. Is there something specific you felt you deserved but didn't get?
options: Credit for work that went unnoticed
A clearer sense of where I stand
More support from the team or leadership
Actually, I'm realizing it wasn't really about that
answer=I chose it — no one asked me to:A2_R_CONTRIBUTION
answer=Someone needed help and I stepped up without thinking:A2_R_CONTRIBUTION
answer=It was expected, but I gave more than required:A2_R_CONTRIBUTION
answer=Honestly, I'm not sure — it just happened:A2_R_CONTRIBUTION
answer=Fair — I put in effort and it showed:A2_R_CONTRIBUTION
answer=Underappreciated — I expected more acknowledgment:A2_R_ENTITLEMENT
answer=Neutral — I wasn't tracking it:A2_R_CONTRIBUTION
answer=Like I could have done more for others too:A2_R_CONTRIBUTION
answer=Credit for work that went unnoticed:A2_R_ENTITLEMENT
answer=A clearer sense of where I stand:A2_R_ENTITLEMENT
answer=More support from the team or leadership:A2_R_ENTITLEMENT
answer=Actually, I'm realizing it wasn't really about that:A2_R_CONTRIBUTION

nodes.text:
Today, you were oriented outward — toward what you could give, not just what you could get.

That kind of discretionary effort — the help that wasn't asked for, the extra that wasn't required — is what teams are actually built on. It doesn't always get noticed. It always matters.

Today, your energy was pulled toward what you deserved — recognition, credit, fairness. That's not wrong. Wanting acknowledgment is human.

But here's a question worth sitting with: was there a moment today where you could have given something — effort, help, attention — without waiting to see if it would be returned?
One more layer. We've looked at how you handled today, and what you gave. Now — how wide was your view?
When you think about today's biggest challenge, who came to mind most?
options: Mostly me — it was my problem to solve
My team — we were all dealing with it
A specific colleague who seemed to have it harder
The people we ultimately serve — users, customers, end users
answer=Mostly me — it was my problem to solve:A3_Q_SELF
answer=My team — we were all dealing with it:A3_Q_WIDE
answer=A specific colleague who seemed to have it harder:A3_Q_WIDE
answer=The people we ultimately serve — users, customers, end users:A3_Q_WIDE

nodes.text: You were focused on your own challenge today. Was there anyone around you who was also struggling — that you noticed but didn't act on?
options: Yes — I saw it but had nothing left to give
Yes — and honestly I didn't think to step in
I wasn't aware of anyone else's situation
No — I checked and no one needed anything

nodes.text: Your concern extended beyond yourself today. Did that wider view change how you acted — or just how you felt?
options: I acted differently because of it — changed my priorities
I felt more connected but didn't change what I did
It helped me put my own stress in perspective
I'm not sure — I just noticed I cared about more than myself
answer=Yes — I saw it but had nothing left to give:A3_R_SELF
answer=Yes — and honestly I didn't think to step in:A3_R_SELF
answer=I wasn't aware of anyone else's situation:A3_R_SELF
answer=No — I checked and no one needed anything:A3_R_ALTROCENTRIC
answer=I acted differently because of it — changed my priorities:A3_R_ALTROCENTRIC
answer=I felt more connected but didn't change what I did:A3_R_ALTROCENTRIC
answer=It helped me put my own stress in perspective:A3_R_ALTROCENTRIC
answer=I'm not sure — I just noticed I cared about more than myself:A3_R_ALTROCENTRIC

nodes.text:
Today, your radius was tight — your own challenge filled the frame. That's understandable. Some days are genuinely consuming.

Maslow's later work suggested that the healthiest humans eventually shift from 'what do I need?' to 'what does the world need from me?' That shift doesn't happen all at once. It happens in small moments — a glance, a question, a small act of noticing.

Tomorrow: one deliberate look outward.

Today, your view extended beyond yourself. That's not nothing — most people spend entire days without genuinely considering anyone else's experience.

The research on well-being is consistent: people who regularly orient toward others — not out of obligation, but out of genuine concern — report higher meaning and lower suffering. You're already doing this. Keep the radius wide.

Here's what today looked like:

On agency — you leaned {axis1_dominant}. On contribution — you leaned {axis2_dominant}. On radius of concern — you leaned {axis3_dominant}.

{summary_reflection}

That's today. Tomorrow is a clean slate.
Rest well. See you tomorrow.

axis1.internal: toward ownership — seeing your hand in what happened
axis1.external: toward circumstance — letting the day happen to you more than through you

axis2.contribution: toward giving — your energy went outward
axis2.entitlement: toward receiving — your energy tracked what you were owed

axis3.altrocentric: outward — others were in your frame
axis3.self: inward — your own challenge filled the view

combinations.internal_contribution_altrocentric: Three for three — agency, generosity, and wide concern. That's a good day, even if it didn't feel like one.
combinations.internal_contribution_self: You owned your day and you gave well. Tomorrow, one deliberate look outward might complete the picture.
combinations.internal_entitlement_altrocentric: You stayed in control and noticed others — but somewhere in the middle, the focus was on what you were owed. Worth noticing.
combinations.internal_entitlement_self: You owned your actions, but today's frame was narrow. What you did mattered. What might it look like to let that ripple outward?
combinations.external_contribution_altrocentric: You gave a lot and cared broadly — but you may not have fully seen your own hand in it. You had more agency today than you think.
combinations.external_contribution_self: You gave to others while feeling like circumstances were in charge. That's a strange mix — generosity without ownership. You were more in control than it felt.
combinations.external_entitlement_altrocentric: You cared about others, but today felt like it was happening to you and that you weren't getting your due. Two of three is still something. The agency piece is the unlocked door.
combinations.external_entitlement_self: Today was tough — you felt reactive, underappreciated, and alone in it. That's a hard combination to carry. Tomorrow, pick one small thing you can control and do it first.
